sourceMovie.title: The American
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The American for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Anton Corbijn keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Godfather Part III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The American clicked for you, The Godfather Part III (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The American?
faq.answer: Try The Godfather Part III, The Brave One, Bordertown—each shares DNA with The American while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The American?
faq.answer: The American (2010) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The American worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
